title: RISC-V Linux syscall subset (asm-generic numbers, -errno returns)

max_fds.doc: Open files a program may hold at once, beyond the three console descriptors. Costs 8 bytes of .bss each.

write_max.doc: Largest single write() serviced. A longer request returns a short count, which is legal and is what Linux does.

path_max.doc: Longest path openat() accepts. A longer one is -ENAMETOOLONG rather than a kernel that reads user memory until it faults; this buffer sits on the trap stack.

io_chunk.doc: Bounce-buffer size read() copies through, also on the trap stack. Larger means fewer filesystem calls per read and a deeper kernel stack.
